title: oplismenus colonum var. walteri
intro: Oplismenus colonum var. walteri, commonly known as Walter's false false pheasant's tail grass, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It is primarily used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. In terms of herbal preparations, Walter's false false pheasant's tail grass is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
